channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Kamala Harris is going all in on Snapchat. But Donald Trump is still banned
item.description: Since July, Kamala Harris’s campaign has purchased more than 5,000 Snapchat ads, amounting to an estimated $​​5.3 million. It’s not hard to understand why: Snapchat is a haven for young people.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Patch your Bitdefender and Trend Micro security software immediately to stay safe from these threats
item.description: Bitdefender Total Security users are advised to be using the newest version of the software to catch vulnerabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Perplexity is sued by Dow Jones and ‘New York Post’ for copyright infringement
item.description: Rupert Murdoch’s Dow Jones, which is the parent company of the Wall Street Journal, and the New York Post both sued AI search startup Perplexity on Monday for infringing copyrighted content. The News Corp.-owned companies accused Perplexity of a “massive amount of illegal copying” of their work.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: State Department making sure change is more than just a name
item.description: Kelly Fletcher, the State Department’s CIO, said the newly named Bureau of Diplomatic Technology is aligning cybersecurity and customer experience. The post State Department making sure change is more than just a name first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How Republicans pushed Silicon Valley social media companies to stop fighting election misinformation
item.description: The shift took place against the backdrop of an intimidation campaign led by Republican state and federal lawmakers aimed at forcing social media companies to platform falsehoods and hate speech and thwarting those working to study or limit the spread of that destabilizing content.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Penguin Random House's AI Warning: A New Chapter in Copyright Concerns
item.description: Penguin Random House includes warning on books regarding copyright and training AI models, sparking concerns among publishers and tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap
item.description: Addressing the federal cybersecurity skills gap requires a multifaceted approach in coordination with the private sector. The post Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Seis obstáculos a la innovación en TI
item.description: Según una encuesta realizada en 2024 por la empresa de servicios profesionales Deloitte, los directores de sistemas de información (CIO, por sus siglas en inglés) consideran que la innovación es la característica más importante que deben aportar a su función, por delante de aportar valor a los resul
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IBM works to address the developer skills gap with AI
item.description: When IBM announced in May 2023 that its generative artificial intelligence tools were in use by its AI assistant, watsonx, to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications by translating COBOL code into Java, it was just the beginning of its efforts to leverage generative AI for developers
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: El Gobierno de Andorra presenta su Cartera de Identidad Digital Ciudadana
item.description: El Gobierno de Andorra da un paso adelante en su plan de transformación digital con la presentación en sociedad de su Cartera Ciudadana de Identidad Digital en el marco de la celebración de Madrid Tech Show.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Donald Trump is losing on TikTok
item.description: TikTok’s influence has become undeniable in the 2024 presidential election, with both camps leaning full force into the idea that if you can’t beat ’em, join ’em. But that plan hasn’t worked out so well for Republican nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Two Virginia congressional candidates debated an AI chatbot. It was shockingly rational
item.description: On Thursday night an AI chatbot participated in a political debate with candidates for a congressional seat, and, despite a few technical glitches, performed well. There was no robot voice, no off-topic tangents, no personal attacks, no odd “they’re eating the dogs” hallucinations.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation
item.description: The Pentagon made some key tweaks to the final CMMC program rule. Several program watchers said the changes should help simplify a complex compliance program. The post DoD ‘fine tunes’ final CMMC program rule, industry turns attention to implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok approved election misinformation ads despite its own ban, says watchdog
item.description: Just weeks before the U.S. presidential election, TikTok approved advertisements that contained election disinformation even though it has a ban on political ads, according to a report published Thursday by the nonprofit Global Witness.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung’s $17 billion Texas chip factory faces another setback. Here’s why
item.description: Samsung Electronics has postponed taking deliveries of ASML chipmaking equipment for its upcoming factory in Texas as it has yet to win any major customers for the project, three people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Recent recalls raise concerns about food safety, but experts credit better regulation and technology
item.description: According to FDA data, nearly 1,200 food products have been recalled so far in 2024. Two hundred products were recalled in August, the highest monthly total this year.
item.dc:creator: Vivian La, The Mercury News

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Augmented reality screens are a feature, not a product
item.description: Apple released a claustrophobic submarine movie for Apple Vision Pro earlier this month, and it gave me a sinking feeling. The movie Submerged was neither augmented reality (AR) nor virtual reality (VR) nor 360-degree video. It was more like 180-degree video.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 Android 15 features you can bring to any phone today
item.description: No Android 15? No problem. You can still enjoy some of the fancy new features from Google’s latest Android version — no matter who made your current device or how old it might be.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: A10 Networks tackles AI threats with platform updates
item.description: A10 Networks is updating its platforms in a bid to help enterprises deal with increasing risks, including those that might come from AI-driven attacks. The improvements to the network security vendor’s technology span multiple products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco pumps up data center networking with AI, large workloads in mind
item.description: Cisco is boosting network density support for its data center switch and router portfolio as it works to deliver the network infrastructure its customers need for cloud architecture, AI workloads and high-performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Hacked robo-vacuums chase owners, yell slurs
item.description: Some customers report the tiny tech tools chased them around their homes and barraged them with profanities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
